bottleneck.evidence: Final evaluate.py --repeat 3: M=16 817.40 us baseline vs 818.12 us solution, M=32 814.11 us baseline vs 817.63 us solution; advisory profile M=16 reports 785.63 us and only 10.8% HBM, indicating fixed TRTLLM runner/JIT launch overhead dominates this tiny decode sweep.

approaches.summary: Added glm52/routed_gateup_nvfp4_decode as a separate ModelOpt-FP4 task using packed uint8 NVFP4 activations and weights, FP8 E4M3 block scales, FP32 scalar scales, and FlashInfer TRT-LLM routed MoE.
approaches.why: The new task is correct and exercises the requested SGLang NVFP4 backend, but solution.py intentionally mirrors reference.py so it cannot beat the live baseline.

approaches.summary: Kept solution.py identical to the SGLang/FlashInfer reference to validate the new task contract before attempting a custom kernel.
approaches.why: Run-to-run noise plus clone/setup variation made the pass-through candidate slightly slower than the cached reference; no algorithmic optimization was attempted yet.

lesson: For a new SGLang NVFP4 MoE task, first validate the exact FlashInfer TRT-LLM packed tensor and scale contract separately from FP8 DeepGEMM. A pass-through baseline is useful for contract validation but provides no speedup; future work must reduce TRTLLM runner overhead or expose a gate-up-only primitive for the M=16/32 decode regime.
transfers_to: GLM-5.2 modelopt_fp4 MoE decode tasks on sm_100
FlashInfer TRT-LLM routed MoE tasks using packed uint8 NVFP4 plus FP8 E4M3 scales
caveats: This session did not implement a faster custom kernel.
Advisory profile numbers are not used as result speedups.
